In this study, we address a fundamental question in computational melt pool modeling:
the accuracy of diffuse interface approaches for reliable predictions of melt pool dynam-
ics. Diffuse models are widely favored for their inherent robustness and straightforward
heat transfer modeling, focusing on realistic material property ratios representative of
employ very small interface thickness values and correspondingly fine mesh resolutions
difference, finite volume, finite element, lattice Boltzmann, or meshfree discretizations,
such models with experimental measurements is reported, as thoroughly analyzed, e.g.,
this shortcoming. While critical model parameters such as laser absorptivity are often
unknown a priori, thus requiring careful calibration, such a calibration procedure can,
of course, not compensate for potential discretization errors due to insufficient interface
the interface temperature. Thus, quantifying the inherent modeling error and the con-
